root_causes.resolution: SessionState now emits the original legacy payload without _bb_event_contract. Kernel v2 retains the authoritative classification, and the private CLI bridge adapter derives the same classification/family/actor/visibility out of band from SessionState's shared registries.

The canonical regeneration pipeline now runs the complete declared DAG in an isolated scratch checkout, validates that complete candidate before accepted writes, promotes the declared write-set transactionally, runs canonical catalog/claim/hash rebinding, and finishes with C4 validation. Backups remain live through final validation and restore all accepted paths after promotion, rebind, or final-C4 failure. Fixed-point remains a two-pass byte comparison at the existing front door.

before_at_base.tail: Legacy assistant_message payload contained extra _bb_event_contract classification metadata.
All three failed at the explicit transaction-contract assertion because base exposed no scratch-to-accepted regeneration transaction.

focused_results.event_leak: green: exact legacy payload shape, authoritative kernel/private-adapter classification, and compilation emitter/event subset
focused_results.regen_transaction: green: candidate rejection preserves accepted bytes; promotion failure rolls back overwritten and newly created paths; successful promotion precedes rebind and final C4; rebind and final-C4 failures restore the accepted write-set; canonical grouping remains on the existing stage graph
focused_results.fixed_point_run: not run by packet, as explicitly prohibited; integration-only

integration_time_steps_remaining: Merge SP2 with the other accepted AM23 packets so the scratch candidate contains the final event bytes, portable inputs, schema/fixture fixes, and current pins.

From the integration checkout, run the existing canonical command once: PYTHONPATH=<integration-checkout> <venv-python> scripts/e4_parity/regen.py fixed-point --json artifacts/conformance/e4_regen_fixed_point.json. This performs the real accepted write-set promotion/rebinding and the second-pass byte comparison; it was intentionally not run in SP2.

Regenerate and retain the WS-J north-star/self-capture evidence produced by that canonical run: self-runtime manifest, kernel-event JSONL, transcript JSONL, source index, catalog bindings, support claims/manifests, node gates, and dependent WS-J evidence records. Verify replay reconstructs at least the required two items after the sideband fix.
Run the integration product-spine and E4 battery gates against the regenerated head; do not hand-edit or separately bless packet hashes.
notes: Code fixes only: SP2 intentionally changed no generated WS-J evidence and performed no pin rebinding.
No fixed-point regeneration was executed in this worktree.
Independent pre-commit recheck found no remaining P0/P1 blockers after shared-registry metadata and full-lifecycle rollback fixes.
